'''Daimla''' was a terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|world]]<ref name="Podracers" /> located in the [[Daimla system/Legends|Daimla system]], a part of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']] [[Kallea sector/Legends|Kallea sector]].<ref name="EAOC" /> It was the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Daimlo]] [[Species/Legends|species]].<ref name="Podracers" />

Young Daimlos underwent a [[Standard year/Legends|year]]-long rite of passage during which they rammed each other into unconsciousness<ref name="Podracers" /> using their newly-grown [[Bone/Legends|skullplates]] and horns.<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|III|367|Zanales, Scorch}}</ref> The Daimlo [[Zanales]] outdid all his peers during one such contest, which elevated him to the status of a hero on his homeworld,<ref name="Podracers" /> and at some point by [[27 BBY/Legends|27 BBY]],<ref name="Date">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of ''[[Jedi Quest: The Dangerous Games]]'', which includes the participation of [[Zanales]] in a [[Podracing/Legends|Podrace]] on the [[Planet/Legends|world]] [[Euceron]], to [[27 BBY/Legends|27 BBY]].</ref> Zanales had become an off-world [[Podracer/Legends|Podracer]] [[Podracer (profession)/Legends|pilot]].<ref name="JQ">''[[Jedi Quest: The Dangerous Games]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
Daimla was indirectly mentioned in the [[2016]] [[StarWars.com]] article "[[Start Your Engines: Star Wars Podracer Drivers|Start Your Engines: ''Star Wars'' Podracer Drivers, Part 2]]," which was authored by [[Kevin Beentjes]].<ref name="Podracers" /> The StarWars.com [[Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion|Online Companion]] to the [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Daimla [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore Daimla itself, in grid square K-21.<ref name="EAOC" />

[[Jason Fry]], co-author of ''The Essential Atlas'', has [[:File:JasonFryAtlas.png|stated]] that the star systems listed in the book's Appendix section, and by extension in the Online Companion as well, are named after the most prominent planets or celestial bodies located in those systems. This article therefore considers the homeworld of the Daimlo species to be named in accordance with the Daimla system.<ref name="Planetnamia">{{Planetnamia|species=[[Daimlo]]|system=[[Daimla system/Legends|Daimla system]]|legends=1}}</ref>
